title: Capture by reference in lambdas used locally
description: Capture by reference in lambdas used locallyAnalyze your codeintentionality-efficientmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEcppcoreguidelinesperformancebad-practicesince-c++11Why is this an issue?More InfoIf the lifetime of the arguments passed to a lambda function is longer than the lifetime of the lambda itself, these arguments can be passed by reference.Doing so avoids copying potentially big objects and it should be preferred over using copy capture.This rule reports an issue if a lambda passed into an algorithm that uses it locally (all algorithms in headers<algorithm>and<numeric>) captures some values.Noncompliant code exampleusing Vec = vector<int>; Vec applyPermutation(const Vec& v, const Vec& permutation) { assert(v.size() == permutation.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Consider capturing by reference [&] instead of by copy when using lambdas locally with STL algorithms.
